reasoning: This reduction kernel performs 1 FLOP (addition) per 4 bytes loaded, yielding an arithmetic intensity of ~0.25 FLOP/byte, which is far below the A10G ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. The kernel streams through 256 MB of input data with minimal computation, making it squarely memory-bandwidth limited. With 4096 blocks × 256 threads = 1M threads and a grid-stride loop, there is ample parallelism and occupancy to saturate the memory subsystem, so DRAM utilization will be high while compute units remain underutilized.
